domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 35 frac. XIX of the SS Interior Regulation explicitly designates the DGE as the RSI National Focal Point and assigns IHR Article 4 duties. Actor fit is strong. Procedure fit is partial: 24-hour availability, internal contact lists, and WHO communication protocols are not codified. Coordination with SRE (DGRI) and COFEPRIS is implied but not operationally specified.

domestic_norm: NOM-017-SSA2-2012, Para la vigilancia epidemiológica
assessment_summary: NOM-017 numeral 6.6.1-6.6.2 reiterates and operationalises the DGE NFP designation at the technical-normative level, confirming DGE as the domestic focal point for PHEIC notifications. Provides operational specificity not found in the Reglamento Interior, but does not fully specify 24-hour communication protocols or backup NFP contacts.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 2 establishes DGE as a deconcentrated administrative unit of SS, providing the institutional-structural basis for the NFP designation in Art. 35. Anchoring is actor-level only; no IHR obligations or procedures are specified here.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 134 establishes the SINAVE and assigns SS the mandate to develop epidemiological surveillance capacity at all levels. Fourteen fracciones cover detection, notification, laboratory confirmation, and information management. Does not explicitly reference IHR Annex 1 benchmarks or time-bound development milestones.

domestic_norm: NOM-017-SSA2-2012, Para la vigilancia epidemiológica
assessment_summary: NOM-017 numerales 4.2-4.3 confirm that DGE and SS are the operational SINAVE actors, and specify governance structure across federal, state, and municipal levels. Regulatory anchoring is direct but the NOM does not define Annex 1A minimum capacity benchmarks or a time-bound capacity development plan.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 35 frac. X assigns DGE operational responsibility for SINAVE, providing the institutional anchor for national surveillance system development. Does not operationalise IHR-specific capacity benchmarks or establish the planning cycle for IHR core capacity assessment and development.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: The DGE is designated as NFP with IHR coordination duties, which would include extension-request reporting under Art. 5(2). However, no domestic provision explicitly establishes the procedure for requesting or reporting capacity-building extensions to WHO, nor assigns this duty formally.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 136 establishes the general notification framework that underpins WHO reporting obligations. No specific provision requires Mexico to report capacity-building extensions or development plans to WHO under IHR Art. 5(2). Anchoring is contextual only.
reviewer_notes: No explicit Art. 5(2) procedure found in corpus.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 136 frac. I explicitly mandates notification of epidemiological events to the SS (and by implication to WHO via the NFP). Fracciones II-IV cover cross-level and cross-sector notification. However, the Annex 2 decision instrument is not domestically codified, and the 24-hour notification clock is not reflected in this article.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 14 of the RLGS Sanidad Internacional explicitly assigns the duty to notify WHO of events meeting IHR notification criteria, referencing international obligations. This is the most direct regulatory anchor for the Art. 6(1) notification obligation. Procedure for Annex 2 risk assessment is not domestically specified.
reviewer_notes: Closest domestic anchor to IHR Art. 6 notification duty.

domestic_norm: NOM-017-SSA2-2012, Para la vigilancia epidemiológica
assessment_summary: NOM-017 numeral 6.6.1-6.6.2 confirms DGE as operational NFP for PHEIC notifications and formalises the WHO notification channel at technical-normative level. Does not specify the Annex 2 decision algorithm or 24-hour reporting clock.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: NFP designation implies ongoing WHO communication responsibilities. Art. 35 frac. XIX attributes IHR coordination duties to DGE as NFP, which encompasses the Art. 6(2) duty of continued reporting. However, no domestic rule specifies the frequency, format, or continuity of post-notification updates to WHO.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 136 establishes the notification obligation but does not address the continuity duty (ongoing updates to WHO after initial notification). Contextual anchoring only.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 136 provides the general notification framework within which unexpected or unusual events would be reported. Frac. I ("epidemiological events") is broad enough to encompass Art. 7 situations. However, there is no domestic provision mirroring the Art. 7 duty to proactively consult WHO before a formal notification determination.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 181 enables SS to declare a national health emergency for extraordinary or catastrophic situations, which could encompass Art. 7 scenarios. However, Art. 181 is a domestic emergency mechanism, not a WHO consultation instrument; it does not operationalise the proactive WHO-sharing duty under Art. 7.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 33 fracs. I-IV assign DGRI the mandate to coordinate international health relations, including WHO engagement. This provides the institutional channel through which Art. 8 consultations would be conducted. No domestic provision specifically operationalises the Art. 8 duty to consult WHO about events not meeting notification thresholds.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: The NFP designation in Art. 35 frac. XIX is the primary actor anchor for Art. 8 consultations. No procedure for non-notifiable event sharing with WHO is domestically codified.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: The NFP (DGE) is the institutional actor designated to handle WHO verification requests including those arising from non-state source reports under Art. 9(2). However, no domestic norm codifies a 24-hour response procedure, internal escalation protocol, or cross-ministry coordination mechanism for this obligation.
reviewer_notes: No domestic 24-hour Art. 9(2) procedure found. Actor only.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: DGE as NFP is the designated actor for WHO verification requests under Art. 10(2). No domestic provision specifies the 24-hour response window, internal authorization chain, or coordination procedure between DGE, SRE-DGRI, and relevant state health authorities.
reviewer_notes: No domestic Art. 10(2) procedure found.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 181 establishes SS authority to deploy emergency response mechanisms, which partially anchors the national-level response capacity required by IHR Art. 13. However, Art. 181 is a reactive emergency power, not a capacity-building mandate. No domestic provision explicitly requires development of IHR Annex 1B response capacities.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 184 fracs. II and V assign SS authority to coordinate emergency health response and deploy resources, providing partial anchoring for IHR response capacity. Does not establish time-bound development plans or link to Annex 1 benchmarks.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 43 frac. IX of the RISS assigns a CENSIDA/CENAPRECE-equivalent unit emergency response duties including territorial deployment and cross-sector coordination, providing an institutional anchor for IHR response capacity at the national level.

No domestic provision requires Mexico to conduct or report a formal self-assessment of IHR core capacities as required by Annex 1A par. 2. The obligation to assess and report to WHO on surveillance and response capacity gaps within two years of IHR entry into force is not reflected in any provision in the current corpus.
reviewer_notes: No domestic self-assessment mandate found. Full gap confirmed.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 134 SINAVE mandate covers detection and notification at all levels including primary care and community levels (fracs. IV, V, VI). This provides partial anchoring for Annex 1A par.4 community-level detection capacities. No provision specifies the minimum capacity benchmarks from IHR Annex 1A par.4.

domestic_norm: NOM-017-SSA2-2012, Para la vigilancia epidemiológica
assessment_summary: NOM-017 numeral 4.2-4.3 operationalises SINAVE governance at federal, state, and municipal levels, providing the regulatory framework for primary-level surveillance capacity. Does not enumerate Annex 1A par. 4 specific requirements.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 146 authorises SS to establish and maintain public health laboratories for epidemiological confirmation, providing partial anchoring for the laboratory confirmation capacities required by Annex 1A par. 5. Does not specify a national reference laboratory network or inter-laboratory quality standards aligned to IHR.

domestic_norm: NOM-017-SSA2-2012, Para la vigilancia epidemiológica
assessment_summary: NOM-017 numerales 3.1.11-3.1.12 designate CONAVE as the technical-epidemiological body for national disease management and surveillance coordination, providing the intermediate-level institutional structure. Does not specify Annex 1A par. 5 minimum capacities.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 134 assigns SS the authority to assess all epidemiological events, underpinning the national-level 48-hour assessment capacity required by Annex 1A par. 6. The article does not specify the 48-hour window or IHR risk assessment methodology.

domestic_norm: NOM-017-SSA2-2012, Para la vigilancia epidemiológica
assessment_summary: NOM-017 numeral 6.6.1-6.6.2 specifically assigns DGE as NFP for PHEIC assessment and notification, providing direct regulatory anchoring for national-level event assessment. The 48-hour response window is not domestically codified.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 181 provides the statutory basis for national emergency health response, including coordination of specialized support resources. Partial anchoring for Annex 1A par. 6 response capacity requirements; no IHR-specific benchmarks.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 43 frac. IX assigns territorial emergency response duties to a designated federal health unit, providing partial anchoring for the national response capacity tier. Does not specify IHR-required specialised support (e.g., medical countermeasures, decontamination) or inter-government coordination protocols.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 26 of the RLGS Sanidad Internacional sets minimum infrastructure and staffing requirements for health services at designated ports and airports, directly corresponding to Annex 1B par. 1 continuous capacity requirements. Partial fit because specific IHR Annex 1B items (e.g., accessible medical facilities, secure areas for traveller assessment) are not fully enumerated.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 27 specifies minimum capacity requirements for PoE health services including permanent health personnel, inspection equipment, and communication. Provides the most detailed domestic anchoring for Annex 1B par. 1 PoE capacity requirements.
reviewer_notes: Most specific domestic anchor for Annex 1B par.1.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 352 LGS establishes the legal basis for international sanitary inspection at PoE and assigns COFEPRIS and SS authority, providing the statutory foundation for PoE capacity requirements. Does not specify Annex 1B minimum capacities.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 26 provisions for PoE infrastructure and services provide partial anchoring for PHEIC-specific PoE capacities. PHEIC-specific escalation procedures (assessment areas, isolation facilities, rapid conveyance inspection) are not explicitly addressed.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 6 authorises health-based entry/exit restrictions and special measures at PoE in emergency contexts, providing partial anchoring for PHEIC-response PoE capacities. Does not specify the full range of Annex 1B par. 2 PHEIC capacities (e.g., referred care pathways, vector surveillance at PoE).

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 352 LGS constitutes the primary statutory basis for Mexico's international sanitary inspection system at PoE, providing partial anchoring for the Art. 19 IHR obligation to develop and maintain Annex 1 PoE capacities. No time-bound capacity development plan is required domestically.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 10 designates and lists the airports, ports, and ground crossings where full IHR Annex 1 capacity requirements apply. Directly implements the IHR Art. 19/20 designation requirement at the regulatory level. Does not specify capacity development timelines.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 10 explicitly lists the designated international airports, seaports, and land border crossings for IHR Annex 1 capacity development, directly implementing the Art. 20(1) designation obligation. The list mechanism is in place; updating procedures and formal communication to WHO of the list are not fully specified domestically.
reviewer_notes: Strongest single domestic anchor for IHR Art. 20 designation.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 352 provides the statutory mandate for international sanitary control at PoE, which is the legal basis underlying the Art. 10 RLGS designation mechanism.
reviewer_notes: Supporting statutory anchor for OBL-019.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 44 assigns duties to conveyance operators and addresses inspection of ships at PoE, providing a contextual anchor for ship sanitation certification. However, specific Ship Sanitation Control Exemption Certificate and Ship Sanitation Control Certificate (SSCEC/SSCC) procedures, formats, and issuing authority are not specified in the corpus.
reviewer_notes: No domestic provision found specifically governing SSCEC/SSCC issuance.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 359 LGS establishes notification requirements for international health events, which provides contextual anchoring for the Art. 20(3) obligation to notify WHO of ports authorized to issue ship sanitation certificates. No domestic provision specifies the WHO notification procedure or the maintenance of an authorized-ports list.
reviewer_notes: No domestic authorized-ports list mechanism found for Art. 20(3).

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 26 includes ground crossing health services in the minimum PoE capacity framework, providing partial anchoring for Art. 21 ground crossing designation. Land border health infrastructure requirements are less detailed than those for airports and ports.
reviewer_notes: Ground crossing provisions less developed than airport/port provisions.

domestic_norm: Ley Aduanera
assessment_summary: Art. 3 Ley Aduanera assigns the SAT-Aduana exclusive competence over ground crossings for customs purposes and mandates coordination with other authorities including SS. Provides a coordination anchor for health authority function at ground crossings, but no health capacity requirements are specified.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 17 Bis frac. IX designates COFEPRIS as the IHR-relevant sanitary authority at PoE, providing the statutory basis for the Art. 22(1) competent authority designation. COFEPRIS-SS coordination for Art. 22 functions is not procedurally specified.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 44 RLGS Sanidad Internacional assigns duties for conveyance inspection, cargo monitoring, and health measures at PoE, directly corresponding to the Art. 22(1) competent authority functions. Specific IHR Art. 22 functions (vector surveillance, decontamination, baggage inspection) are partially addressed.

domestic_norm: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
assessment_summary: Art. 14 CPEUM guarantees due process and prohibits deprivation of rights without fair legal process, providing the constitutional constraint on compulsory health measures under IHR Art. 23(3). Rights safeguard fit is strong at the constitutional level, but specific procedural guarantees for travellers subject to health measures are not operationalised in implementing legislation.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 139 LGS authorises isolation, quarantine, and other health security measures and specifies the legal conditions for their application. Provides partial rights-safeguard anchoring through the requirement for legal justification. Does not address the specific Art. 23(3) requirement that no health measure be applied without the traveller's informed assent and adequate information.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 69 RLGS governs isolation procedures including traveller treatment, providing partial anchoring for the duty to inform travellers of health measures and alternatives under IHR Art. 23(4). Does not specify the information-provision obligation or the right to refuse.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 70 RLGS governs quarantine procedures including traveller notification, providing contextual anchoring for the duty to inform travellers. Does not explicitly address the IHR Art. 23(4) right to informed consent before prophylaxis.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 44 is the primary domestic anchor for conveyance operator obligations, assigning duties for vessel/aircraft health compliance and data provision to authorities. IHR Art. 24(1) specific operator obligations (carriage of sick persons, decontamination cooperation, provision of health documents) are partially addressed.

domestic_norm: Ley Aduanera
assessment_summary: Art. 7 Ley Aduanera requires advance passenger and cargo information from conveyance operators, providing an indirect anchoring for the data-sharing obligations of conveyance operators. The health-specific obligations of conveyance operators under IHR Art. 24(1) are not addressed.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 68 lists the health security measures available to authorities at PoE including inspection, disinsection, deratization, and decontamination of conveyances, providing partial anchoring for Art. 27(1) measures applicable to affected conveyances. The provision does not specify the procedural steps or traveller treatment requirements for affected conveyance scenarios.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 69 governs isolation procedures for persons from affected conveyances, providing anchoring for the traveller component of IHR Art. 27(1). Specific conveyance-health measures procedure is not fully developed.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 69 provides the domestic procedure for isolating travellers who may pose a public health risk, partially anchoring Art. 30 public health observation obligations. The specific IHR Art. 30 right of the suspect traveller to continue travel while under observation is not addressed.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 70 quarantine provisions provide regulatory anchoring for public health observation of suspect travellers. Does not specify the IHR Art. 30 traveller right to travel onward to another State Party that assumes observation responsibilities.

domestic_norm: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
assessment_summary: Art. 14 CPEUM prohibition on non-consensual deprivation of rights provides the constitutional floor against compulsory invasive health measures. However, no domestic provision directly mirrors the IHR Art. 31(1) presumption that invasive measures shall not be required for entry or travel.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 139 LGS provides the statutory authorization for health security measures and implicitly constrains their application to legally justified circumstances, providing partial rights safeguard anchoring. Does not explicitly prohibit compulsory invasive measures without consent as specified in IHR Art. 31(1).

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 139 LGS authorises health security measures including detention of suspected cases where there is a public health risk, providing partial anchoring for the Art. 31(2) authority to deny entry or apply least-invasive alternatives when a traveller refuses required health measures.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 68 lists available health security measures at PoE, providing the regulatory toolkit for application of alternative measures when travellers refuse health examinations. Does not specify the balancing test or procedural safeguards required by IHR Art. 31(2).

domestic_norm: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
assessment_summary: Art. 1 CPEUM establishes the human dignity and non-discrimination principles that underpin the IHR Art. 32 obligation to treat travellers with respect, dignity, and without discrimination. Constitutional anchoring is strong but no implementing health-sector regulation specifically addresses traveller dignity in health measure contexts.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 69 isolation provisions include procedural requirements for how isolation is to be carried out, providing partial regulatory anchoring for the IHR Art. 32 treatment standards. The specific IHR Art. 32 requirements (adequate food/water, communication with family/consular officials, appropriate medical treatment) are not specified.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 9 RLGS corresponds to IHR documentation requirements at PoE and governs which health documents can be required from ships and aircraft. Provides partial anchoring for the Art. 35 prohibition on requiring health documents other than those provided in the IHR. A comprehensive domestic prohibition on non-IHR health documents is not explicit.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 3 RLGS Sanidad Internacional establishes the WHO/IHR normative hierarchy as directly applicable, which contextually anchors the IHR Annex 6 vaccine standards for prophylaxis under Art. 36. However, no domestic provision specifically requires vaccines administered under IHR to conform to Annex 6 specifications or WHO recommendations.
reviewer_notes: No specific Annex 6 domestic implementation found.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 9 RLGS lists required maritime health documentation and governs its use at designated ports, partially anchoring the Art. 37 Maritime Declaration of Health obligation. Specific form, content requirements, and submission timing for the Maritime Declaration are not specified in the domestic corpus.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 9 RLGS governs aviation health documentation at designated international airports, partially anchoring Art. 38 Aircraft General Declaration requirements. Art. 5 RLGS definition of aeronautical terms provides definitional anchoring. Specific form and completion requirements for the Health Part are not domestically codified.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Sanidad Internacional
assessment_summary: Art. 44 covers conveyance inspections and health measures at PoE, contextually anchoring the ship certificate framework. Specific provisions on the 6-month validity period, exemption certificate duration, renewal procedures, and recognition of foreign certificates under IHR Art. 39 are not found in the corpus.
reviewer_notes: No domestic SSCEC/SSCC issuance or renewal procedure found.

assessment_summary: No domestic provision found that specifically prohibits charging travellers for personal health measures (examination, vaccination, prophylaxis, health documents) required under IHR Art. 40. The corpus does not contain a traveller health measures fee framework.
reviewer_notes: Full gap. No domestic Art. 40 implementation found.

domestic_norm: Ley Aduanera
domestic_article: Art. 56, párrafo 4 (regulaciones y restricciones no arancelarias / sanidad)
assessment_summary: Art. 56 para. 4 Ley Aduanera references health-based non-tariff restrictions on goods and cargo, providing a contextual anchor for the regulatory framework within which health charges on goods might be assessed. No domestic provision establishes a published schedule of charges for health measures applied to goods, cargo, or containers as required by IHR Art. 41.
reviewer_notes: No domestic charge schedule for Art. 41 implementation found.

domestic_norm: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
assessment_summary: Art. 1 CPEUM prohibits discrimination on any ground including nationality and health status, providing the constitutional non-discrimination mandate that underpins IHR Art. 42. Constitutional anchoring is strong; no health-sector implementing provision specifically addresses the non-discriminatory and prompt initiation requirements for health measures.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 139 LGS conditions health security measures on legal justification and proportionality, providing partial anchoring for the Art. 42 prompt and non-discriminatory application requirement. No explicit non-discrimination clause or timeliness standard for health measures is specified.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 181 LGS authorises SS to declare emergency health measures beyond the standard framework, providing partial anchoring for the Art. 43(1) authority to implement additional health measures achieving equivalent or greater protection. The requirement that such measures be science-based, non-unduly-burdensome, and proportionate to health risk is not domestically specified.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 184 fracs. I-V provide the emergency health measures toolkit (quarantine, isolation, movement restriction, vaccination campaigns), partially anchoring the Art. 43(1)-(2) additional measures authority. Does not specify the IHR-required scientific basis, proportionality, and WHO notification duties that accompany additional measures.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: The NFP (DGE) is the institutional actor through which Art. 43(3)+(5) reporting obligations to WHO would be discharged. No domestic provision establishes a procedure for notifying WHO of additional health measures implemented beyond IHR requirements, nor for the 48-hour reporting window under Art. 43(3).
reviewer_notes: No domestic Art. 43(3)/(5) reporting procedure found.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 184 implicitly requires that emergency health measures be reviewed and lifted when the health risk no longer justifies them, contextually anchoring the Art. 43(6) review obligation. No domestic provision specifies a 3-month periodic review of additional IHR measures or the WHO reporting duty triggered by such review.
reviewer_notes: No domestic Art. 43(6) review procedure found.

domestic_norm: Reglamento Interior de la Secretaría de Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 33 fracs. I-IV assign DGRI the mandate to coordinate bilateral and multilateral health cooperation, engage with WHO, and manage international technical assistance requests, providing institutional anchoring for Art. 44(1) collaboration obligations. No provision specifies the types of collaboration (surveillance, response, access to medical countermeasures) required by Art. 44(1).

domestic_norm: Ley Orgánica de la Administración Pública Federal
assessment_summary: Art. 28 LOAPF assigns SRE the competence to negotiate and represent Mexico in international agreements, providing the institutional anchor for treaty-based collaboration under IHR Art. 44. The health sector-specific coordination dimension is addressed through DGRI (Art. 33) rather than SRE alone.

domestic_norm: Ley General de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de Sujetos Obligados
assessment_summary: Art. 16 LGPDPPSO establishes the emergency exception for processing health-related personal data while maintaining safeguards, directly anchoring the IHR Art. 45 framework for collecting and using personal health information collected under IHR obligations. Strong rights safeguard fit; coordination with data subject rights procedures is partial.
reviewer_notes: Strongest single domestic anchor for IHR Art. 45.

domestic_norm: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
assessment_summary: Art. 16 CPEUM establishes the constitutional right to data protection, providing the apex constitutional anchor for the Art. 45 obligation to protect personal health data. Constitutional rights are strong; operational data management rules are governed by LGPDPPSO.
reviewer_notes: Supporting constitutional anchor for OBL-044.

domestic_norm: Reglamento de la Ley General de Salud en Materia de Investigación para la Salud
assessment_summary: Art. 11 RLGS Investigación addresses international research collaboration including the sharing of biological samples and research results, providing a weak contextual anchor for IHR Art. 46 obligations on facilitation of access to biological substances and diagnostic reagents. No domestic provision specifically implements the IHR Art. 46 obligation to facilitate import/export of biological substances for public health response purposes.
reviewer_notes: No specific Art. 46 domestic implementation found in corpus.
